conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Honestly, I’ve been really down. I try to focus on the day ahead, but the fatigue just wears me out.
You mentioned the fatigue wears you out—how has that been affecting your day-to-day routine?
It makes everything a chore. I used to enjoy simple things like walking the dog, but now it feels like climbing a mountain.
Can you tell me more about what that experience has felt like for you?
It’s like watching someone else live my life. I look at people moving around, and I feel like I’m just stuck on the sidelines.
You mentioned feeling stuck on the sidelines—has that made it hard to care about things you used to enjoy?
Yeah, it has. I used to love reading, but now it feels pointless. I don't know why I'm wasting my time with it when I can't even enjoy it.
When you say reading feels pointless, what does that make you think about yourself?
It makes me think I’m done. Like, I've lived my life and now I’m just going through the motions.
When did this sense of emptiness start feeling this strong?
It’s been building for a while. After the divorce, things changed, and I couldn’t get the old me back.
What parts of life feel most difficult or empty lately?
The evenings. I sit there, and I have nothing to look forward to. No one to call, no plans... just silence.
How do you see the next few weeks unfolding for you?
I don't know. I feel like I'm just waiting for something to happen, but I have no idea what that is.
How much of this feels like giving up versus feeling exhausted?
I don't know if it's giving up or just accepting that this is my life now. Either way, it feels like the end of something, even if I don't know what that something is.
Do you feel like people would be better off without you, or is it more that you feel stuck?
I don't think anyone would notice if I wasn't here. It's not about anyone else, it's just... I don't have anything left to give, and I don't know how to fix that.
Can you tell me more about how that has been affecting your day-to-day?
It makes me want to just disappear. I look at people who are still building their lives, and I realize I missed my chance.
How have you been feeling lately?
I have been really down lately. It’s been a few weeks, and I just can’t seem to shake the feeling that I should be doing better by now.
Has that feeling of being down affected your sleep at all?
Yeah, it has. I wake up in the middle of the night and just lie there thinking about all the things I could have done differently.
When you're lying awake at night thinking about those things, does it make it harder to get excited about your day ahead?
Definitely. I wake up feeling drained, so the idea of going to work and just going through the motions is really exhausting.
You mentioned going through the motions at work—has that feeling of not having much energy spilled over into things you used to enjoy doing?
Yeah, it has. I used to love going for a run, but now it just feels like too much effort. I just don't have the energy to do things like that anymore.
When you're lying awake thinking about things you could have done differently, what kind of things come to mind?
Just the usual stuff. I keep thinking I should have taken the job in the city, or maybe I should have said something to her when things were still good.
When you think about those missed opportunities, how does it make you feel about where you are now?
It makes me feel like I've missed out on something. I look at my friends and they’re all moving forward, and I’m just stuck here thinking about what could have been.
When you say you feel stuck, what does that feel like day to day?
It’s just this constant weight on my chest. I feel like I should be doing better, but I don't know how to make it happen.
When you feel that weight on your chest, does it ever make you feel disappointed in yourself?
Yeah, it does. I keep thinking I should be stronger than this, but I just can’t seem to shake this feeling of failure.
When you feel that weight and disappointment, do you find yourself getting more easily frustrated or on edge with people around you?
Yeah, definitely. I get snappy with my colleagues, and I know it’s not them, but I just can’t seem to turn it off.
When you feel that sense of failure, does it make it hard to focus on tasks you need to get done?
It makes it impossible. I sit there staring at my screen, but I just can’t seem to bring myself to do anything because I’m too busy thinking about how I messed up.
When you're sitting there staring at your screen, does it ever make you feel like it's just too much to even start?
Yeah, it does. Sometimes I just get up and walk away because the thought of going back to that desk and dealing with it all is just too much.
